location.hint: Search for a city or use your current location.
location.notSupported: Geolocation is not supported by your browser.

descriptions.alosHashachar: First light, with the sun 16.1° below the horizon — the degree equivalent of a 72-minute dawn, so it tracks the same twilight at any latitude or season.
descriptions.alos72: A fixed 72 minutes before sunrise (the time to walk 4 mil at 18 min each; Rambam and Rishonim) — the dawn the Magen Avraham uses for his proportional-hour zmanim.
descriptions.misheyakir115: Earliest tallit and tefillin — sun 11.5° below the horizon (≈52 min before sunrise in Jerusalem near the equinox; the actual offset varies by place and season).
descriptions.misheyakir11: Sun 11° below the horizon (≈48 min before sunrise in Jerusalem near the equinox; the actual offset varies by place and season).
descriptions.misheyakir102: Sun 10.2° below the horizon (≈45 min before sunrise in Jerusalem near the equinox; the actual offset varies by place and season).
descriptions.sunrise: Sunrise (HaNetz). The vatikin complete the morning Shema just before this moment and begin the Amidah exactly at sunrise.
descriptions.sofZmanShmaMGA: End of the 3rd proportional hour by the Magen Avraham — the day measured from dawn (72 min before sunrise) to nightfall (72 min after sunset), an earlier deadline.
descriptions.sofZmanShmaGRA: End of the 3rd proportional hour by the Vilna Gaon (GRA) — the day measured from sunrise to sunset.
descriptions.sofZmanTfilaMGA: End of the 4th proportional hour by the Magen Avraham — day measured from dawn (72 min) to nightfall (72 min).
descriptions.sofZmanTfilaGRA: End of the 4th proportional hour by the Vilna Gaon (GRA) — day measured from sunrise to sunset.
descriptions.chatzos: Solar noon — the midpoint of the day, halfway between sunrise and sunset.
descriptions.minchaGedola: Earliest time to pray Mincha — 6½ proportional hours into the day (a half-hour after midday).
descriptions.minchaKetana: Preferred time to pray Mincha — 9½ proportional hours into the day.
descriptions.plagHamincha: 10¾ proportional hours into the day (1¼ hours before sunset) — the earliest one may pray Maariv or accept Shabbat.
descriptions.candleLighting: Candle lighting before Shabbat / Yom Tov — the set minutes before sunset (18 by default; 40 is the Jerusalem custom).
descriptions.sunset: Sunset (Shkia) — Shabbat / Yom Tov begins, and the doubtful twilight (bein hashmashot) starts.
descriptions.tzaisGeonim: An early, lenient nightfall — sun 5.95° below the horizon (the Geonim / Baal HaTanya, ≈24 min after sunset in Jerusalem near the equinox). Some rely on it to end rabbinic fasts.
descriptions.tzais: Three small stars are visible — sun 8.5° below the horizon (≈36 min after sunset in Jerusalem near the equinox). A slightly stringent nightfall; the new day begins.
descriptions.tzais72: Rabbeinu Tam's stringent nightfall — a fixed 72 minutes after sunset (4 mil × 18 min). Relied on for ending Shabbat, Yom Tov, and Yom Kippur.
descriptions.chatzosLaila: Solar midnight — the halachic midpoint of the night, halfway between sunset and sunrise.

baseDescriptions.alos: Dawn — first light; the start of the halachic day for many mitzvot.
baseDescriptions.misheyakir: Earliest time to don tallit and tefillin — when there is enough light to recognize an acquaintance.
baseDescriptions.sofZmanShma: Latest time to recite the morning Shema — the end of the third proportional hour.
baseDescriptions.sofZmanTfila: Latest time for the morning Amidah (Shacharit) — the end of the fourth proportional hour.
baseDescriptions.tzais: Nightfall — three stars are visible and the new halachic day begins.

panel.allPassed: All of today's zmanim have passed.

settings.title: Settings
settings.candleOffsetHint: Minutes before sunset (40 is common in Jerusalem).

footer.disclaimer: Zmanim times computed with <link>kosher-zmanim</link>. For halachic decisions, consult your local rabbinic authority.

city.metaDescription: Today's halachic zmanim (Jewish prayer times) for {city}: sunrise, sof zman shma, chatzot, mincha, candle lighting, sunset and nightfall.
city.heading: Zmanim for {city}, {country}
city.indexDescription: Find halachic zmanim (Jewish prayer times) for cities around the world.
city.indexLede: Pick a city for today's prayer times, or open the calendar to choose any location.

home.metaDescription: Accurate halachic zmanim (Jewish prayer times) and calendar for any location, with Hebrew and civil dates.
